definition: A registered mechanism that turns a parsed element tree plus a change source into committed DOM updates, satisfying the CustomRenderStrategy contract (mount, optional update, optional dispose). Capability is feature-detected by which optional methods are present — a strategy without update is mount-once. The four registered strategies are declarative-static (the native-first default), vdom, fine-grained, and imperative. The sibling of Change Strategy: change strategies detect what mutated, render strategies commit how the DOM reflects it.
usage: Render Strategy Protocol (Web Components). A technical contract, not a UX preference — surfaces under /protocols/, never /intents/. The JSX adapter and the declarative <component> element are consumers that target a chosen strategy.
